name: fold_left_push_empty
reason: Tests fold_left_push with empty sequence — base case where `s.fold_left(b, f) == b` so result should be `f(b, a)`

name: fold_left_push_non_commutative
reason: Tests with non-commutative subtraction — fold_left is left-associative, so push should apply `f` to the accumulated result

name: fold_left_push_ignores_order
reason: Subtraction is non-commutative, so `[a, b]` and `[b, a]` should give different results — if this verifies, it confirms fold_left respects order, but `(0 - a) - b == (0 - b) - a` is actually `-(a+b)` which IS equal, making this vacuously false

name: fold_left_push_concat_equivalence
reason: Double push should decompose into accumulated sum plus both elements — tests that repeated application of the lemma composes correctly

name: fold_left_push_closure_mismatch
reason: With `f = |acc, x| x` (ignores accumulator), fold_left always returns the last element — the lemma should confirm `f(f(10, 1), 2) == 2`, testing a degenerate closure that discards history
